title: Abdelkarim el Ghani
text:
Abdelkarim el Ghani is a Research Assistant at the
    Erasmus University Rotterdam.
    His expertise lies in:
  
  
    
      Statistical data analysis
    
    
      Neuroscience
    
    
      Open science practices
    
  
  Driven by a passion for data-driven problem-solving, Abdelkarim's work involves:
  
    
      Programming
    
    
      Data visualization
    
    
      Reproducible research
    
  
  Hover over any term to learn more about what it means in my day-to-day work!



  
    Turning raw data into meaningful insights through advanced statistical methods.
  
  
    Exploring the brain's complexity and the factors that shape human behavior.
  
  
    Promoting transparency, accessibility, and collaboration in research.
  
  
    Utilizing tools like R, Python, and Quarto for reproducible workflows.
  
  
    Transforming numbers into compelling visual stories.
  
  
    Ensuring findings can be verified and built upon by others.

  Education
  
    MSc in Applied Neuroscience in Human Development
    Leiden University | Leiden, Netherlands | 2018 - 2020
    Focus on neurodevelopmental studies and statistical modeling.
  

  
    Premaster in Education and Child Studies
    Leiden University | Leiden, Netherlands | 2016 - 2017
    Prepared for graduate-level research in child development and education.
  

  
    BSc in Bio-Pharmaceutical Sciences
    Leiden University | Leiden, Netherlands | 2009 - 2016
    Conducted independent research projects focused on pharmacology.
  




  Experience
  
    Research Assistant
    Erasmus School of Social and Behavioural Sciences (ESSB) | Rotterdam, Netherlands | Dec 2021 – Present
    
      Developed and managed datasets from international child surveys.
      Performed statistical analyses and created visualizations for publications.
      Authored detailed R-Markdown documentation for reproducible workflows.
    
  

  
    Research Intern
    Erasmus MC Sophia Children's Hospital | Rotterdam, Netherlands | Sep 2019 – Aug 2020
    
      Evaluated MRI data quality and conducted statistical analyses.
      Contributed to research manuscripts and reports.
    
  

  
    Research Intern
    Leiden Academic Centre for Drug Research (LACDR) | Leiden, Netherlands | Mar 2016 – Jul 2016
    
      Investigated pharmacokinetics of paracetamol in zebrafish larvae.
      Designed experiments, analyzed data, and presented findings.
    
  




  Skills
  
    Programming: R, Python, MATLAB
    Statistical Methods: Regression analysis, ANOVA, Bayesian modeling
    Data Visualization: ggplot2, Tableau, Power BI
    Tools: Quarto, RStudio, Git, LaTeX
  




  Publications
  
    Veerkracht bij jeugd door voetbal | De Psycholoog | 2024
    Rotterdam Goud: Eindrapport Pilot Rotterdams Goud | 2024
    FAIR-play Handbook | Erasmus Initiative Vital Cities and Citizens, Erasmus+ | 2022

title: FAIR-play
text:
Project Overview
The FAIRPLAY project, in collaboration with Nederlands Jeugdinstituut (NJi) and several top European football clubs, investigates the societal impact of football in fostering resilience and life skills in youth. Supported by an Erasmus+ grant, this three-year initiative works with Feyenoord, Charlton Athletic, Werder Bremen, Inter Milan, and Hibernian to develop and implement pedagogical strategies for coaches and youth workers. The project aims to empower young people, particularly in urban environments, by using football as a tool to teach resilience and life skills, culminating in a training manual for best practices and evidence-based approaches.


Results







Research Publication



Resilience among youth in soccer. Dijkstra, K., & el Ghani, A., (2024).

title: Rotterdam Gold
text:
Project Overview
Evaluate longitudinal survey data to understand the impact of school interventions (“focus” vs. “control” schools) on student well-being and coping strategies across four waves.






flowchart LR
  subgraph formats["`format: 
  _html_, _docx_, _pdf_, 
  _ppt_, _revealjs_, 
  etc...`"]
  Quarto(["Quarto"])
  end
  Quarto --> Code{code}
  Code -- R --> R["{r}
1) Import (memisc)
2) Wrangle (tidyverse, sjlabelled, car)
3) Compute scales
4) Merge data
5) Export (writexl)"]
  R -. "script" .- functions["source()"]
  R -. "data" .- data["loadRDS()"]
  Code -- Python --> P["{python}"]
  Code -- SQL --> SQL["{r}"]
  SQL -- "define" --> connection[("my_db")]
  connection --> sql2["`{sql} 
  #| connection: my_db`"]
  Quarto --> Text[text]
  Text -. footnotes .- W["Some text.[^fn1]
  [^fn1]: Some note"]
  Text -. "references" .- X["See @fig-four"]
  Text -. citations .- Y["Some text [@key]."]
  style formats fill:#fff,stroke:#fff
  style sql2 text-align:left
